results.title: Dune: The Context-Aware Mac Keypad That Reclaims Your Workflow | FunBlocks AI Reviews
results.content:
In the era of AI-driven development and non-stop remote collaboration, "context switching" is the silent productivity killer. Every time you toggle between VS Code, a browser-based AI agent like Claude, and a video conferencing app, you lose momentum. Dune enters the hardware scene as a clever, context-aware keypad designed specifically for Mac users who want to regain that lost time. [...] At its core, Dune is a sleek, three-button hardware peripheral that sits unobtrusively next to your primary keyboard. Unlike standard macro pads that require manual layer switching, Dune is intelligent. It senses which application is in the foreground and automatically updates its key assignments to match the tasks you are most likely performing in that specific environment. It is a bridge between the physical world and your digital workspace, tailored for developers, power users, and anyone [...] ### Key Features & Highlights

Dune distinguishes itself through deep integration and simplicity. The device isn’t just a collection of buttons; it is a smart controller that understands the unique language of the tools you use most.

results.content:

Create shortcuts just by talking to Claude, browse a Marketplace of community-built scripts, and assign any workflow to your three keys across the apps you use. results.title: Dune: Context-aware Mac keypad to automate workflows + meetings | ChatGate
results.content:
At the core, Dune watches the active app on your Mac and remaps its three physical keys in real time. According to the official Dune page, those keys can become GitHub review actions, VS Code shortcuts, Claude triggers, meeting controls, URLs, or custom scripts depending on what you are doing. The broader Project Mirage homepage makes the same pitch and adds a few practical details: Dune is USB-C powered, macOS-only, and built as a compact aluminum accessory rather than a desk-sized console. [...] The strongest use cases are developer workflows and online meetings. The official site says Dune can map actions for GitHub, VS Code, Claude, and Openclaw, while the Dune page shows meeting-specific flows such as one-click joins for Zoom, Microsoft Teams, and Google Meet after calendar sync is enabled. It also advertises dedicated mic and camera controls, plus a one-click unmute action that brings the meeting window to the foreground. [...] ChatGate
Try ChatGate

Dune: Context-aware Mac keypad to automate workflows + meetings

# Dune: Context-aware Mac keypad to automate workflows + meetings

Dune is a hardware idea with a clear software thesis behind it. On the official Project Mirage site and the dedicated Dune product page, the company positions it as a three-key macOS keypad that changes behavior based on the app in front of you, so it works more like a live context surface than a fixed macro pad.

results.title: Dune - Context-aware Mac keypad to automate workflows + meetings | ProductCool
results.content:
Definition: Dune is a specialized context-aware external keypad and tactile interface designed exclusively for the macOS ecosystem. Categorized as an adaptive input peripheral, it features three physical keys housed in a premium CNC-machined enclosure, functioning as a real-time hardware shortcut controller that dynamically reconfigures its input commands based on the active foreground application. [...] Key Innovation: The specific innovation lies in the integration of "Intent-Based UI." By combining hardware tactile feedback with real-time application state monitoring, Dune transforms from a static input device into a proactive workflow assistant that anticipates the next logical step in a user's professional pipeline.

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) [...] High-Performance Industrial Hardware: Constructed from CNC-machined anodized aluminum, Dune is a bus-powered USB-C device weighing 50g. Its ultra-compact dimensions (40mm x 10mm x 10mm) allow it to sit adjacent to a standard laptop or desktop keyboard. Because it is USB-C powered and lacks an internal battery, it offers reduced latency and high reliability for mission-critical developer environments.

### Problems Solved
